title: Kammia

{{Quote|The Imperial Colonization Board regrets to announce that the Kammia colony, Drynn system, has succumbed to a mysterious virus. At this time, there are no reports of survivors.|Imperial HoloVision news release|<ref name="TAB" />}}
'''Kammia''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the extremely remote [[Drynn system]], among the sparsely populated [[Shiritoku Spur]] of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. Kammia was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] colony world of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], notable for its rich soil. In the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] ceded a number of colony worlds, including Kammia, to the [[Ssi-ruuvi Imperium]] in exchange for the [[Alien/Legends|aliens']] [[Species/Legends|species]]-[[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] [[entechment]] technology. The Empire released a news report claiming that a mysterious virus had wiped out the Kammia colony to hide the truth.

==Description==
Kammia was a particularly remote [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Drynn system]]<ref name="TAB" /> of the [[Shiritoku Spur]],<ref name="GATORW" /> an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that lay on the edge of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TAB" /> in the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] region known as [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="Atlas" /> Kammia had excellent soil, rich for agricultural purposes.<ref name="TAB" />

==History==
===Sacrificed to the Ssi-ruuk===
One of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] frontier [[Human/Legends|Human]] colonies on the periphery of known space,<ref name="TAB" /> Kammia was settled during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the Empire<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|197|Kammia}}</ref> and was dedicated to producing agricultural [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodstuffs]] due to the planet's rich soil. The Imperial Human colonists introduced [[Chandrilan/Legends|Chandrilan]] grain to the planet's soil in their agricultural efforts.<ref name="TAB" />

Following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Ssi-ruuvi Imperium]], representing a [[Ssi-ruu|species]] of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] invaders from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], began to attack Imperial colony worlds along the edge of the Outer Rim to assimilate the Human colonists inhabiting them. Through a process known as "[[entechment]]," the alien invaders harnessed their captive Humans' life forces as a source of energy for their war machines.<ref name="TAB" />

News of the invasions soon reached [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Craving the aliens' entechment technology for his purposes, the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] agreed to cede Imperial citizens to the aliens in return for access to their technology.<ref name="TAB" /> One of the first colony worlds sacrificed to the aliens was Kammia,<ref name="CSWE" /> which fell following a [[Raid on Kammia colony|raid]] on the outpost.<ref name="GATORW" /><ref name="TAB" />

===Imperial cover-up===
As had been done with the aliens' first invasions, the Empire easily hid the truth of the Kammia colony's fate through the control of [[Media/Legends|media]] outlets. The Empire created and distributed false cover stories for each of the lost colony worlds, ranging from industrial accidents to [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] attacks.<ref name="TAB" />

On [[Great ReSynchronization|37:4:21]],<ref name="TAB" /> in the year [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date1">37:4 in the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system corresponds to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] in the [[Galactic Standard Calendar]], as explained [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> the [[Imperial HoloVision]] released a news report explaining that a mysterious disease had wiped out the Kammia colony with no survivors<ref name="TAB" /> and that the planet was placed under strict quarantine.<ref name="CSWE" /> According to the [[Imperial Colonization Board]], the release explained, the belief was that the colonists' Chandrilan grains had interacted with components in the planet's soil to produce a non-indigenous [[Plant/Legends|plant]] that led to the evolution of a contagious "mystery virus," which quickly spread to the colony. The ICB expressed its condolences to the families of those lost colonists, and the Emperor pledged that the [[Imperial Survey Corps/Legends|Imperial Survey Corps]] would refine its soil-screening process to avoid further tragedies.<ref name="TAB" />

In the years following, rumors spread through the region of aliens attacking [[Starship/Legends|starships]] in the Shiritoku, and [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] tales suggested that the destruction of colonies such as Kammia was related. Those rumors proved true when the aliens [[Bakura Incident|invaded]] the Imperial planet [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]]<ref name="GATORW" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="Date2">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Ssi-ruuvi Imperium|Ssi-ruuvi]] [[Bakura Incident|invasion]] of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The colony world Kammia was first introduced into ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in the [[February]] [[1996]] ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Kathy Tyers]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]], which identified Kammia as one of the first Imperial planets to succumb to Ssi'ruuvi attack.<ref name="TAB" /> The [[2004]] sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' later mentioned Kammia briefly in its description of the planet Bakura, establishing both planets to be in the Shiritoku Spur region of Wild Space.<ref name="GATORW" />

The [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion]], a supplement to the [[2009]] publication ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', includes in its database a "Kammia [[Star system/Legends|system]]," located in Wild Space; there is no listing for the Drynn system,<ref name="Atlas" /> Kammia's [[Star/Legends|star]] system, as originally established by ''The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook''.<ref name="TAB" /> However, according to ''The Essential Atlas'' co-author [[Jason Fry]], the Online Companion's database listed star systems according to names of well-known celestial objects rather than proper system names, and that the database's listing is not intended to create new system names or override previously established continuity.<ref name="TFN">{{JCFCite|url=posts/30422084|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=Senator_Cilghal|date=2009-09-11|time=5:52 PM|quote=okay, I specifically asked Jason. He confirmed that the names in the System column are well-known celestial objects at that location, NOT proper system names; these are not meant to create new system names nor override those from pre-existing EU (ie, the name of [[Azurbani system]] has NOT been changed to [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]] system, nor [[Ten Tempests system]] to [[Vortex/Legends|Vortex]] system, etc.).|archiveurl=https://archive.is/FdKJi|archivefile=File:JasonFry-JCFAtlas.png}}</ref> Given Fry's declaration, this article treats the "Kammia system" as conjecturally synonymous to the Drynn system.
